title: Arthratherum hygrometricum
intro: Arthratherum hygrometricum, commonly known as the hygro-metric grass, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating various infections. The bioactive constituents of Arthratherum hygrometricum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Arthratherum hygrometricum is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Chloridoideae and the tribe Cynodonteae. The genus Arthratherum is part of the large and diverse family Poaceae. Arthratherum hygrometricum is native to tropical Africa and Madagascar. The classification of Arthratherum hygrometricum reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants within the same family and subfamily.
